title: coccothrinax miraguama subsp. havanensis
intro: Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. havanensis, also known as the Cuban thatch palm, is a rare and endemic subspecies of palm tree found only in the Zapata Swamp region of Cuba. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. havanensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
